question.text: Manish does not receive HRA from his employer and pays monthly rent of Rs.18,000. His total income before Section 80GG deduction is Rs.6,00,000. Under the old regime, what is the maximum deduction he can claim under Section 80GG?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Section 80GG deduction (for those not receiving HRA) is the least of: (a) Rent paid minus 10% of total income = Rs.2,16,000 - Rs.60,000 = Rs.1,56,000; (b) Rs.5,000 per month = Rs.60,000 per annum; (c) 25% of total income = Rs.1,50,000. Least of (Rs.1,56,000, Rs.60,000, Rs.1,50,000) = Rs.60,000. **The Trap:** Rs.1,50,000 picks 25% of TI without comparing all three. Rs.2,16,000 takes full rent paid. Rs.1,56,000 takes rent minus 10% of TI. The Rs.5,000/month cap is often the binding constraint and is easy to overlook. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series X-A tests Section 80GG as an alternative to HRA exemption for self-employed individuals and those without HRA. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The Rs.5,000/month cap under 80GG has not been revised for years and is usually the binding constraint — this makes 80GG a relatively modest deduction compared to HRA exemption for most taxpayers.
